bullets.text: Q3 combined ratio of 91.9 with 12% NPW growth; YTD CR of 90.4 with 13% NPW growth, marking the 15th consecutive quarter of double-digit NPW growth at margins well above the 96 CR target.

YTD auto frequency declined 2-3% while severity rose 6-7%; bodily injury severity led at plus 8% YTD; collision severity moderated to plus 3% in Q3 vs. plus 7-8% in H1; Commercial Lines reserves strengthened in select states.
Companywide NPW up 13% YTD; Commercial Lines NPW grew 16% YTD at an 88.7 CR; transportation network company partnership expanded to Lyft in Nevada, Illinois, and Pennsylvania effective October 1.

Property rates increased in hail-prone states; minimum wind/hail deductibles implemented; new severe convective storm loss models adopted; Property 4.0 product live in three states with three more planned for Q4.
71% of Progressive employees engaged per Gallup index, well above 34% U.S. workforce average; 84% employee survey response rate, up 8 percentage points since 2017 Gallup baseline.

Investment portfolio earned 1.2% in Q3; fixed income and equities both performed positively; credit risk in fixed income reduced as valuations became less favorable; portfolio grew toward $39 billion at year-end.

BOP performing well in Ohio with first rate revision filed producing a rate decrease; expansion to second state planned for November; auto product 8.6 launched in Iowa in Q2 then live in six additional states in Q3.

CEO signed Business Roundtable's new Statement on the Purpose of a Corporation alongside 181 CEOs, aligned with Progressive's longstanding stakeholder-first approach encompassing customers, employees, suppliers, communities, and shareholders.
